clusters.name: Cross-lingual Emotion Detection
clusters.description: This research focuses on detecting emotions in text across multiple languages using techniques like knowledge distillation, large language models, and domain adaptation. It addresses challenges such as language dependency, data scarcity, and the need for interpretable multilingual systems.
clusters.trend_reason: Recent paper output is clearly above its historical baseline.

clusters.name: LLM Alignment and Preference Optimization
clusters.description: This research focuses on improving Large Language Models through techniques like Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF), Direct Preference Optimization (DPO), and steering vectors to enhance helpfulness, safety, and controllability. It addresses challenges such as alignment tax, bias, and the need for personalized or multilingual adaptation.
clusters.trend_reason: Recent paper output is clearly above its historical baseline.

clusters.name: Speculative Decoding
clusters.description: Speculative Decoding accelerates Large Language Model inference by using a smaller draft model to generate candidate tokens, which are then verified by a larger target model in parallel. This approach significantly reduces the computational cost per token while maintaining generation quality.
clusters.trend_reason: Recent paper output is clearly above its historical baseline.

clusters.name: LLM Safety and Red Teaming
clusters.description: This research focuses on identifying vulnerabilities in Large Language Models through adversarial attacks, jailbreak prompts, and automated red-teaming methods to evaluate and improve their safety alignment. It encompasses both offensive techniques to bypass safety filters and defensive strategies to enhance model robustness against malicious inputs.
clusters.trend_reason: Recent paper output is clearly above its historical baseline.

clusters.name: Retrieval-Augmented Generation
clusters.description: This direction focuses on enhancing Large Language Models by integrating external knowledge sources via retrieval mechanisms to improve accuracy, reduce hallucinations, and adapt to diverse domains. It involves optimizing retrieval strategies, evaluating system robustness, and resolving conflicts between parametric and contextual knowledge.
clusters.trend_reason: Recent paper output is clearly above its historical baseline.

clusters.name: Narrative Analysis and Character Role Extraction
clusters.description: This research direction focuses on automatically identifying, classifying, and analyzing narratives, character roles, and entity framing within news articles and social media texts using NLP techniques. It increasingly leverages Large Language Models (LLMs) and hierarchical classification strategies to handle multilingual and multi-label narrative detection tasks.
clusters.trend_reason: Recent paper output is clearly above its historical baseline.

clusters.name: LLM Watermarking and Linguistic Steganography
clusters.description: This research focuses on embedding imperceptible, robust markers into text generated by Large Language Models to verify authorship, protect intellectual property, and ensure content provenance against paraphrasing or translation attacks. It encompasses both white-box weight perturbation methods and black-box linguistic steganography techniques.
clusters.trend_reason: Recent paper output is clearly above its historical baseline.

clusters.name: Patronizing and Condescending Language Detection
clusters.description: This research focuses on developing NLP models to identify language that is patronizing or condescending, particularly towards vulnerable communities. It involves creating annotated datasets, fine-tuning pre-trained transformers, and exploring various classification strategies to detect such harmful linguistic patterns.
clusters.trend_reason: Recent paper output is clearly above its historical baseline.

clusters.name: Multilingual NLP
clusters.description: This research direction focuses on developing and evaluating natural language understanding and generation capabilities across multiple languages, particularly addressing low-resource scenarios through few-shot learning, cross-lingual transfer, and large-scale multilingual datasets. It investigates how multilingual pretraining and instruction tuning enhance model performance in diverse linguistic contexts.
clusters.trend_reason: Recent paper output is clearly above its historical baseline.

clusters.name: Commonsense Validation and Explanation
clusters.description: This research focuses on enabling NLP models to validate statements against commonsense knowledge and generate explanations for their reasoning. It primarily involves leveraging pre-trained language models and knowledge resources to improve comprehension and generation tasks.
clusters.trend_reason: Recent paper output is clearly above its historical baseline.

clusters.name: Semantic Textual Relatedness
clusters.description: This research direction focuses on quantifying the semantic relationship between sentence pairs, often involving multilingual evaluation, embedding methods, and transformer-based models. It aims to develop robust predictors for determining whether texts are semantically related, similar, or unrelated.
clusters.trend_reason: Recent paper output is clearly above its historical baseline.

clusters.name: Retrieval-Augmented Generation Security
clusters.description: This research focuses on identifying and mitigating vulnerabilities in Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems, including adversarial attacks, privacy breaches, and robustness issues. It explores techniques to secure the retrieval and generation pipelines against malicious inputs and data poisoning.
clusters.trend_reason: Recent paper output is clearly above its historical baseline.
